name: Jordan Vineyard & Winery
description: Iconic French-inspired estate known for elegant Cabernet and Chardonnay. All visits include food pairings prepared by their culinary team. One of Sonoma's most celebrated luxury experiences.

name: Dry Creek Vineyard
description: Pioneer of Dry Creek Valley, first winery after Prohibition in 1972. Famous for Zinfandel and Sauvignon Blanc. Features bocce courts, picnic grounds, and classic California charm.

name: Gloria Ferrer Caves & Vineyards
description: First sparkling wine house in Carneros, founded 1986. Spanish heritage meets California terroir. Stunning terrace views and méthode champenoise sparkling wines.
tagline: Don't Count the Days, Make the Days Count

name: Scribe Winery
description: MEMBERS ONLY. Historic hacienda setting with natural wines. Must join allocation list for visits. Garden snacks included with tastings.

name: Nalle Winery
description: Hidden gem specializing in elegant, lighter-style Zinfandel. Family-owned since 1984, often poured by the Nalle family themselves. Wine Spectator Top 100.
tagline: One of the Best Zinfandel Makers in California

name: Iron Horse Vineyards
description: Premier sparkling wine estate in Green Valley. Served at White House state dinners. Outdoor tastings with stunning vineyard views. The Oyster Girls visit weekends.

name: Martinelli Winery & Vineyards
description: Family farming since 1880s. Historic hop barn tasting room. Famous Jackass Hill old-vine Zinfandel from impossibly steep slopes.

name: DeLoach Vineyards
description: Pioneering biodynamic winery since 1975. Beautiful gardens, chickens, and oak-shaded picnic areas. Franco-American tasting compares Sonoma and French wines.

name: Unti Vineyards
description: Beloved for Italian and Rhône varieties. Casual Airstream tasting bar, bring-your-own-picnic vibe. Organic farming, traditional methods, serious wines without pretense.
tagline: Wine with a Sense of Place

name: Benziger Family Winery
description: Pioneering biodynamic winery with famous tram tour through vineyards. Great for families with peacocks roaming the property. Educational and engaging.

name: Flowers Vineyards & Winery
description: Stunning modern architecture in the Russian River Valley. Known for exceptional Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from extreme coastal vineyards.

name: Seghesio Family Vineyards
description: Italian heritage since 1895. Downtown Healdsburg tasting room with bocce courts. Famous for old-vine Zinfandel and Italian varietals.

name: Halleck Vineyard
description: Small luxury estate. Best Pinot in America three years running. Intimate 16-guest max tastings include vineyard tour and food pairings. Dog-friendly.

name: Pedroncelli Winery
description: Family-owned since 1927, survived Prohibition. Fourth generation, 70% woman-owned. Best values in Dry Creek Valley. Unpretentious and welcoming.

name: Arista Winery
description: Beautiful Japanese garden setting. Small-lot Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. Family-owned with meticulous attention to detail. Peaceful, refined tasting experience.

name: Landmark Vineyards at Hop Kiln Estate
description: Historic stone hop kiln building, California Historical Landmark. Expansive lawn for picnics. Great Pinot Noir and Chardonnay with unique character.

name: Wilson Winery
description: Century-old tin barn, one of oldest structures in Dry Creek. Big, ripe Zinfandels. Fantastic deck views. Picnic and charcuterie plates available.

name: Buena Vista Winery
description: California's first premium winery, founded 1857. Historic caves, costumed 'Count' tours. Champagne-focused Bubble Lounge. Rich history with a playful spirit.

name: Ridge Vineyards Lytton Springs
description: Legendary 1970s Zinfandel Renaissance producer. Environmentally-friendly tasting room built from straw bales. Serious wines, serious reputation.

name: Gary Farrell Vineyards & Winery
description: Stunning hilltop views of Russian River Valley. Elegant, refined Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. Sophisticated tasting experience with knowledgeable staff.

name: Ferrari-Carano Vineyards & Winery
description: Grand Italian-inspired estate in Dry Creek Valley with stunning gardens, underground barrel cellars, and award-winning wines. Known for elegant hospitality and beautiful grounds.

name: Chalk Hill Estate Winery
description: Exclusive estate in its own AVA with 1,300 acres. Known for exceptional Chardonnay and estate hospitality. Beautiful grounds with culinary gardens.

name: Sebastiani Vineyards & Winery
description: Historic Sonoma winery founded 1904. One of California's oldest family-operated wineries. Downtown Sonoma tasting room with rich heritage.

name: Chateau St. Jean
description: Beautiful 1920s chateau estate with rose gardens, redwood grove, bocce courts, and grand lawn views of Sugarloaf Ridge. One of Sonoma Valley's most scenic properties.
tagline: A Fine European Estate Under Sonoma Skies

name: Roth Estate
description: Alexander Valley estate focused on Bordeaux varietals since 2001. Also produces cool-climate Sonoma Coast wines. Modern, approachable tasting experience.

name: Banshee Wines
description: Hip, modern tasting room on Healdsburg Square. Known for cool-climate Pinot Noir and Chardonnay with a laid-back, urban wine bar vibe.

name: Lancaster Estate
description: Ultra-premium Alexander Valley estate producing limited-production Bordeaux-style wines. Appointment-only experiences in elegant setting.

name: Kendall-Jackson Wine Estate & Gardens
description: Ultimate wine, food, and farm-to-table destination. Beautiful sensory gardens, culinary demonstrations, and America's #1 Chardonnay. Great for families and foodies.

name: La Crema Estate at Saralee's Vineyard
description: Cool-climate Pinot Noir and Chardonnay specialist. Beautiful Russian River Valley estate with vineyard views. Known for approachable, food-friendly wines.

name: Stonestreet Estate Vineyards
description: High-elevation Alexander Mountain Estate producing exceptional Cabernet and Chardonnay. Rugged mountain vineyards with unique terroir.

name: Matanzas Creek Winery
description: Bennett Valley pioneer famous for stunning lavender fields (peak bloom June) and exceptional Sauvignon Blanc and Merlot. Peaceful, scenic destination.

name: Hartford Family Winery
description: Small-lot, single-vineyard Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from old vines. Part of Jackson family but independently run. Serious wines, intimate setting.

name: Copain Wines
description: Rhône and Burgundian varieties from cool Mendocino and Sonoma Coast sites. Laid-back, relaxed tasting in a beautiful setting. Known for restraint and elegance.

name: Siduri Wines
description: Pinot Noir obsessives with fruit from top vineyards across California and Oregon. Urban winery tasting room in Healdsburg.

name: Fort Ross Vineyard & Winery
description: Spectacularly sited tasting room above the fog with Pacific Ocean views. Only estate tasting room in Fort Ross-Seaview AVA. Exceptional Pinot Noir, Chardonnay and rare Pinotage.
tagline: Where the Mountain Meets the Sea

name: Red Car Wine
description: Sonoma Coast specialist producing expressive, site-driven Pinot Noir and Syrah. Casual tasting room in Sebastopol with passionate, knowledgeable staff.

name: Gundlach Bundschu Winery
description: One of California's oldest family-owned wineries (1858). 320 acres at intersection of four AVAs. Known for excellent value and laid-back atmosphere with live music.

name: Domaine Carneros
description: Stunning French château producing méthode traditionnelle sparkling wines. Owned by Taittinger Champagne. Elegant terrace overlooking vineyards.

name: Cline Cellars
description: Historic Carneros estate with California Missions museum. Known for Rhône varieties and old-vine Zinfandel. Free tastings, picnic grounds, farm animals.

name: Schug Carneros Estate Winery
description: German-style winery founded by legendary winemaker Walter Schug. Known for exceptional Pinot Noir and Chardonnay in Carneros tradition.

name: Ram's Gate Winery
description: Luxurious modern estate at southern Carneros gateway. Farm-to-table restaurant, stunning architecture, premium Pinot and Chardonnay.

name: Kunde Family Winery
description: Five-generation family estate in Sonoma Valley with 1,850 acres. Cave tours, hiking trails, stunning views. Known for estate wines and outdoor activities.

name: St. Francis Winery & Vineyards
description: Mission-style winery known for exceptional food and wine pairings. Famous multi-course pairings with Sonoma County chef-prepared cuisine.

name: Ledson Winery & Vineyards
description: Stunning 16,000 sq ft Gothic-style castle known as the 'Castle' of Sonoma Valley. Grand architecture, manicured grounds, diverse wine portfolio.

name: Imagery Estate Winery
description: Sister winery to Benziger known for unusual varietals and artist series labels. Over 180 original artworks on display. Organic, biodynamic farming.

name: Keller Estate
description: One of few wineries with tasting room in Petaluma Gap AVA. Cave tours, stunning views, exceptional cool-climate Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.

name: McEvoy Ranch
description: 550-acre olive oil and wine estate in hidden private valley. Organic farming, lavender gardens, Italian varietals alongside Pinot and Syrah.

name: Merry Edwards Winery
description: Legendary winemaker's estate producing acclaimed Russian River Pinot Noir and Sauvignon Blanc. Beautiful tasting room with vineyard views.

name: Korbel Champagne Cellars
description: Historic 1882 winery in redwood forest along Russian River. America's best-known sparkling wine. Free marketplace tastings, tours of historic buildings.

name: Lynmar Estate
description: Ultra-premium Russian River Pinot Noir and Chardonnay estate. Stunning modern architecture, farm-to-table cuisine, exceptional hospitality.

name: Twomey Cellars
description: Part of Silver Oak family focusing on terroir-driven Pinot Noir. Modern tasting room on Westside Road with intimate, seated experiences.

name: Balletto Vineyards
description: 100% estate-grown wines from family that transformed vegetable farm to vineyards. Relaxed patio garden, friendly staff, excellent value wines.

name: Porter Creek Vineyards
description: Organic, biodynamic pioneer producing elegant Pinot Noir and old-vine Carignane. Intimate family winery, often poured by the winemaker.

name: A. Rafanelli Winery
description: Legendary cult Zinfandel producer. Four generations of family winemaking. Appointment only, limited production, often sells out quickly.

name: Preston Farm & Winery
description: Organic farm and winery with bread from wood-fired oven, olive oil, and organic wines. Rustic farmstead aesthetic, great picnic destination.
tagline: A Taste of the Good Life

name: Mauritson Wines
description: Sixth-generation Sonoma farming family now making wine. Known for powerful Rockpile and Dry Creek Zinfandels. Gorgeous hilltop tasting room.

name: Lambert Bridge Winery
description: Beautiful covered bridge entrance leads to charming Dry Creek Valley estate. Known for Bordeaux varietals and excellent picnic grounds.

name: Bella Vineyards & Wine Caves
description: Cave tasting room built into hillside. Zinfandel specialist with old-vine wines from Dry Creek and Alexander Valley. Intimate underground experience.

name: Alexander Valley Vineyards
description: Historic estate on site of Cyrus Alexander's 1840s homestead. Cave tours, organic wines, excellent value. Sin Zin and CYRUS are popular wines.

name: Silver Oak Alexander Valley
description: Iconic Cabernet Sauvignon producer with stunning Alexander Valley estate. LEED Platinum certified, glass-enclosed tasting room, library wines.

name: Francis Ford Coppola Winery
description: Filmmaker's wine country resort with pools, movie memorabilia, restaurant, and entertainment. Fun, family-friendly destination beyond just wine.

name: Rodney Strong Vineyards
description: Sonoma County pioneer with wines from multiple AVAs. Architecturally striking winery with concerts and events. Broad portfolio, solid values.

name: MacRostie Winery & Vineyards
description: Elegant estate specializing in Chardonnay and Pinot Noir from cooler Sonoma sites. Beautiful modern tasting room with vineyard views.

name: J Vineyards & Winery
description: Sparkling and still wine specialist. Bubble Room for sparkling flights. Multi-course tasting menu at the Legacy Bar. Russian River elegance.

name: Cline Family Cellars
description: Historic 1850s farmstead in Carneros. California mission museum on property. Rhône varietals and old-vine Zinfandel. Free tastings.

name: Bella Vineyards and Wine Caves
description: Cave tasting experience in Dry Creek Valley. Zinfandel focus from old-vine vineyards. Intimate, memorable cave setting.

name: Medlock Ames
description: Organic estate on Bell Mountain. Historic renovated farm buildings. Known for Cabernet and Bell Mountain red blend.

name: Vérité Estate
description: Ultra-premium Bordeaux-style wines with 17 perfect 100-point scores. Stunning abbey-inspired estate by Jackson Family Wines and French vigneron Pierre Seillan. Three blends: La Muse, La Joie, Le Désir.
